title: toxicodendron aucheri
intro: Toxicodendron aucheri, also known as poison sumac, is a flowering plant native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. The plant contains urushiol, a resin that causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in humans. Despite its toxic properties, Toxicodendron aucheri has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as a tea or ointment, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential for causing severe allergic reactions.
